coreIssue: Bill, as an Acting Coach at the Director level, seems to be struggling with understanding and meeting director-level expectations. His experience might not fully align with the demands of his current role, which could be affecting his performance and confidence. The meeting should focus on clarifying what is expected from him at this senior level, identifying any gaps in skills or knowledge, and discussing strategies to bridge these gaps effectively. It's crucial to provide him with a clear roadmap so he can align his efforts with organizational goals.
openingQuestion: Bill, can you walk me through your understanding of the key responsibilities and expectations at the director level in your role?
listenFor: whether he identifies specific director-level responsibilities
if they express uncertainty about expectations
whether he mentions any support needed
avoid: do not assume he knows all director-level expectations
do not overlook his potential strategic insights
goodOutcome: You will have a mutual understanding of director-level expectations and a plan to address any gaps Bill identifies.
suggestedAction: Agree on a follow-up meeting in two weeks to review progress and adjust the development plan as necessary.
